mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in less than 50% of rate-cut months.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer demand, causing S&P 500 forward returns to turn negative over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 65% of instances where unemployment rises above the specified threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP growth and stable consumer price inflation indicates robust economic demand without monetary policy tightening pressure, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in more than 55% of quarters, or the interaction term loses statistical significance with p greater than 0.10 in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in the index of industrial production reflect expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side momentum, which translates to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 60% of months with rising industrial production, or the signal predictive power vanishes after controlling for market momentum.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month decline in retail sales indicates a sudden contraction in consumer spending, which reduces corporate revenue expectations and forces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 returns are positive in more than 70% of months following a retail sales drop exceeding the specified threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms broad-based labor market tightening, boosting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal fails to predict positive returns in more than 50% of quarters, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated consumer price inflation combined with near-zero policy rates creates expectations of imminent monetary tightening, which compresses equity multiples and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in more than 60% of periods where inflation exceeds the threshold while policy rates remain low, or the signal loses predictive power once Fed forward guidance is priced in.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in housing starts signal strengthening residential investment and broader economic confidence, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days through improved sector earnings expectations.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 65% of months with rising housing starts, or the signal information coefficient drops below 0.01 across rolling 2-year windows.
